We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other concerned Municipal Officials allocate 1% of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of Local Council for the Protection of Children and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of the Republic Act 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to immediately liquidate the cash advances for payroll of previous years and those in the current year which are due for liquidation in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the procurement process prescribed in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184 and its relevant Annex/Appendix.  We further recommend the submission of the lacking procurement documents.

We recommended that concerned officials be required to submit immediately the lacking documents to support the liquidation report on social pension fund as required by Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and DSWD Administrative Order No. 04, series of 2012.

We recommended that management strictly require concerned officials to submit copies of contracts, purchase orders and job order contracts within the prescribed period in adherence to the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and to ensure that contracts are certified as funded by proper authorities before these are perfected or executed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular reconciliation of the balances of the bank and book records and to prepare regularly the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same to the audit team to afford verification.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant to submit supporting details/documents to the credit adjustments pertaining to prior years reconciling items that were booked up in CY 2013.

We recommended that management strictly comply with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts under the three funds and book
the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement.

Further, the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor transactions
affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different
funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission. Also, whenever possible, settle
immediately transactions between funds to minimize efforts in monitoring and
reconciliation and more significantly avoid erroneous account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
journal entries so as to present the correct account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in both the
Statement of Financial Position and the related Notes to the Financial Statements the
current portion of the Municipality’s long-term debt in accordance with paragraphs
70 and 80 of PPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
ensure that the implementation of the LGSF-ADM projects be within its targeted
timeline and strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-03.
Furthermore, we recommend that the Mayor require the Accountant to submit the
paid disbursement vouchers and the contract pertaining to the project in compliance
with the same circular.

We recommended that henceforth the Municipal Mayor in coordination with
the Municipal Social Welfare & Development Officer and other concerned officials
ensure the full utilization of the fund transfer in accordance with DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 02 and its accompanying Memorandum of Agreement to
attain maximum benefits. Refrain from the use of the general fund for the same
purpose while the trust fund is still available during the year.

Finally, we recommended that management immediately refund the
remaining balance of P317,000.00 to DSWD FO 8.

We recommended that the LGU ensure that only those projects eligible to be
funded out of the 20% Development Fund are included in its budget and its
subsequent implementation in compliance with the provisions of DILG and DBM
JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the updating and review
of the LDRRMFIP to incorporate the previous years’ unexpended balance in the
Special Trust Fund. Likewise, transfer the exact balance of Unspent Current Year
DRRM Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommend that the officials comprising the Local School Board be
directed to strictly follow the mandate of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1,
series of 2017 particularly on the drafting and approval of the SEF Budget. Likewise,
the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Accountant prepares demand letters
for the immediate liquidation of Cash Advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other concerned
Municipal Officials allocate 1% of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation
of the programs, projects and activities of Local Council for the Protection of

and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
